intro: Ink is free and open source while Unreal Engine runs on usage-based pricing. Ink: Open-source narrative scripting language by Inkle Studios powering 80 Days, Heavens Vault, and Disco Elysium. Has a JSON runtime and Unity/Godot plugins for interactive fiction. Unreal Engine: Epic Games AAA-grade game engine powering blockbuster titles and film VFX. Features Nanite geometry, Lumen GI, MetaHuman, Blueprint visual scripting, and a free-to-use royalty model. Ink fits narrative-heavy games where writers own script files and version control. Unreal Engine fits studios making 3D games where cinematic fidelity is central to the pitch. On our rubric Ink scores 4/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency, while Unreal Engine scores 5/5 and 3/5. The honest trade-off: Ink's main drawback — Syntax takes getting used to for non-writers; Unreal Engine's — 5% royalty kicks in past $1M and grows fast. One point in Ink's favour: Proven on shipped hits like Disco Elysium and 80 Days. For Unreal Engine: Nanite and Lumen deliver film-grade visuals at runtime.
quick_take: Ink is for narrative-heavy games; Unreal Engine is for studios making 3D games; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Ink if narrative-heavy games where writers own script files and version control, and if you want to avoid recurring license costs and are willing to self-host.
verdict_b: Choose Unreal Engine if studios making 3D games where cinematic fidelity is central to the pitch, and if usage-based billing fits your traffic profile better than a flat subscription.

faq.q: Is Ink cheaper than Unreal Engine?
faq.a: Yes on list price: Ink is free and open source, while Unreal Engine runs on usage-based pricing. Keep in mind that Ink shifts costs to hosting, ops, and engineering time. Total cost of ownership can flip depending on your team's capacity.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Ink to Unreal Engine?
faq.a: Migrating out of Ink is straightforward — it's rated low lock-in (OSS scripting language). The harder question is how deeply you commit to Unreal Engine afterward, since it sits at medium lock-in (Content tightly tied to engine). Plan the forward cost, not just the exit.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Ink or Unreal Engine?
faq.a: Unreal Engine scores higher in our rubric (5/5 vs 4/5 for Ink). Transparency is 3/5 for Unreal Engine and 5/5 for Ink. DX scores are rubric-based, not benchmarks, so evaluate against your own toolchain.

faq.q: Is Unreal Engine a good alternative to Ink?
faq.a: They sit in the same category, so yes — Unreal Engine is a plausible alternative for many Ink use cases. It fits best when studios making 3D games where cinematic fidelity is central to the pitch. Skip it if 2D, small-scope, or low-spec mobile games where Unity and Godot excel.
